audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for prophecy/tongues, building the assembly, intelligibility, order, women silence textual/pastoral complexity, all things decently/orderly.

verses.restored: Pursue love, and desire spiritual things/gifts, especially that you may prophesy.
For the one speaking in a tongue does not speak to humans but to the Divine; for no one hears/understands, but in Spirit one speaks mysteries.
But the one prophesying speaks to humans building up, encouragement, and consolation.
The one speaking in a tongue builds oneself, but the one prophesying builds the assembly.
I wish all of you to speak in tongues, but more that you prophesy; greater is the one prophesying than the one speaking in tongues, unless one interprets, so that the assembly may receive building.
Now, siblings, if I come to you speaking in tongues, what will I benefit you unless I speak to you in revelation, knowledge, prophecy, or teaching?
Even lifeless things giving sound, whether flute or lyre, if they do not give distinction in notes, how will what is played be known?
For if a trumpet gives an unclear sound, who will prepare for battle?
So also you, unless through the tongue you give a clear word, how will what is spoken be known? For you will be speaking into air.
There are perhaps so many kinds of voices in the world, and none is voiceless.
If then I do not know the power/meaning of the voice, I will be a foreigner to the speaker and the speaker a foreigner to me.
So also you, since you are zealous for spirits/spiritual things, seek that you may abound for building up the assembly.
Therefore let the one speaking in a tongue pray that one may interpret.
For if I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays, but my mind is unfruitful.
What then is it? I will pray with the spirit, and I will also pray with the mind; I will sing praise with the spirit, and I will also sing praise with the mind.
Otherwise, if you bless in spirit, how will the one filling the place of the uninstructed say the Amen at your thanksgiving, since that one does not know what you say?
For you indeed give thanks beautifully/well, but the other is not built up.
I thank the Divine, I speak in tongues more than all of you;
but in assembly I desire to speak five words with my mind, so that I may instruct others also, rather than ten thousand words in a tongue.
Siblings, do not become children in thinking; in harmfulness be infants, but in thinking become mature/complete.
In the Law it has been written: By people of other tongues and by lips of others I will speak to this people, and not even thus will they hear me, says the Lord.
So then tongues are for a sign, not to those trusting but to the untrusting; but prophecy is not for the untrusting but for those trusting.
If therefore the whole assembly comes together in the same place and all speak in tongues, and uninstructed or untrusting people enter, will they not say that you are mad?
But if all prophesy, and some untrusting or uninstructed one enters, one is exposed by all, examined by all.
The hidden things of one's heart become manifest, and so falling on the face, one will worship the Divine, declaring that the Divine is truly among you.
What then is it, siblings? When you come together, each has a psalm, teaching, revelation, tongue, interpretation. Let all things happen for building up.
If anyone speaks in a tongue, let it be by two or at most three, and each in turn, and let one interpret.
But if there is no interpreter, let one be silent in assembly, and speak to oneself and to the Divine.
Let two or three prophets speak, and let the others discern.
But if something is unveiled to another sitting by, let the first be silent.
For you are all able to prophesy one by one, so that all may learn and all may be encouraged.
And spirits of prophets are subject to prophets.
For the Divine is not of disorder but of peace. As in all the assemblies of the holy ones,
let the women be silent in the assemblies, for it is not permitted to them to speak, but let them be subject, just as also the Law says.
But if they want to learn anything, let them ask their own husbands at home, for it is shameful for a woman to speak in assembly.
Or did the word of the Divine go out from you? Or did it arrive to you only?
If anyone thinks oneself to be prophet or spiritual, let one recognize that what I write to you is a command of the Lord.
But if anyone is ignorant, let that one be ignorant.
So then, my siblings, desire to prophesy, and do not forbid speaking in tongues.
But let all things happen beautifully/well and according to order.

translation_notes: Chapter 14 continues the chapter 13 love-rule: gifts are evaluated by whether they build the assembly.

Verses 34-35 must be read with caution alongside 1 Corinthians 11 where women pray and prophesy. Interpretive options include local disruption, quoted Corinthian slogan, or later interpolation; restored layer preserves the text while noting tension.
